data.description:
## Goal
Graduate `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py` (currently 1093 LOC, the single LARGEST module-size offender) off the module-size gate by extracting a cohesive cluster of 10 pure/IO leaf UTILITY symbols into a NEW sibling leaf module `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_helpers.py`.

## Seam (on the code's CURRENT structure)
The 10 moved symbols are the pass's leaf support utilities — NONE of them take `_PassContext`; they are pure/IO helpers that the staying `_PassContext`-driven phase helpers call: `StatusMappingError`, `preflight_status_mapping`, `_commit_binding_store_snapshot`, `_audit_log_probe`, `route_inbound_probe`, `_read_local_tickets`, `_build_filter_target_set`, `_mutation_matches_filter`, `_build_plan_entries`, `_NoOpSyncLogger` (~412 LOC of bodies). The leaf reproduces the `lazy_load`+`_load` bootstrap boilerplate (identical pattern to the sibling `run_differs.py`) so it lazy-loads ITS OWN siblings (config.py, alert_store.py, mutation.py, inbound_probe.py, git_adapter). The leaf imports NOTHING from reconcile.py (true leaf, no back-edge). reconcile.py loads the leaf once at module top via `_helpers = _load('reconcile_helpers', 'reconcile_helpers.py')` and binds each of the 10 names as a re-export.

## DEVIATION from docs/architecture.md remedy row (stated plainly)
The arch.md offenders-table row for reconcile.py proposes a `reconcile_passes.py` seam that extracts the `_PassContext`-driven pass-phase helpers (`_load_snapshots`/`_handle_corrupt_snapshot`/`_apply_mutations`/`_persist_and_log`). That seam is SUPERSEDED: it would require THREE modules to avoid a back-edge, because those phase helpers depend on the leaf utilities being extracted here — so the phase helpers cannot move cleanly without either dragging the utilities along or importing back into reconcile.py. Leaf-utility extraction is the cleaner single split. This ticket DELETES the stale reconcile.py remedy row from docs/architecture.md.

## Nature of change
A pure/mechanical refactor (move + re-export, no signature or logic changes). E5 testing-plan is not-applicable — the existing reconciler unit tests are the oracle (many load reconcile.py by-path and access these symbols as `reconcile.<symbol>` attributes, which the re-exports preserve).

## Acceptance criteria (observable, in-session verifiable)
1. `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile_helpers.py` is in [100,800] (~475).
2. `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py` is <=800 (~670).
3. reconcile.py removed from BOTH `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt`; its remedy row deleted from `docs/architecture.md`; reconcile_helpers.py NOT added to either gate file.
4. `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q` passes.
5. `python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler -q` passes (existing tests are the oracle; all 10 symbols remain resolvable as `reconcile.<symbol>` via re-export).
6. `make lint` reports All checks passed.
data.title: Split reconcile.py — extract pass leaf utilities into reconcile_helpers.py (graduate off module-size gate)
